message:
Close multiple beads after verification + fmt fixes

Closed beads:
- rich_rust-6os: LRU cache for color parsing complete
- rich_rust-0y1: Thread safety verification complete (20 tests pass)
- rich_rust-agw: Rustdoc comments complete (cargo doc clean)
- rich_rust-yz6: Performance EPIC complete (caching implemented)
- rich_rust-2oy: Documentation EPIC complete (6 examples exist)
- rich_rust-zxq: Example programs complete
- rich_rust-6dt: String allocation optimization assessed

Also includes rustfmt fixes to src/style.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-koa","title":"Validate Syntax Highlighting Module (feature-gated)","description":"Validate the Syntax module (requires "syntax" feature flag) against Python Rich Syntax behavior.

FEATURE FLAG: syntax (requires syntect crate)

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Syntax struct construction and configuration
2. Theme support and customization
3. Line number rendering
4. Language detection and explicit selection
5. Code block borders and padding

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Syntax::new() accepts source code and language
- [ ] Theme selection (default themes work)
- [ ] Line numbers option (show_line_numbers)
- [ ] Line range selection (start_line, end_line)
- [ ] Word wrap behavior within code blocks
- [ ] Background color styling
- [ ] Indent guide characters (if supported)
- [ ] Tab expansion to spaces
- [ ] SyntaxError type for failures

RENDERING TESTS:
- [ ] Renders to styled Segments correctly
- [ ] Colors match syntect theme
- [ ] Line numbers aligned properly
- [ ] Handles unknown languages gracefully

EDGE CASES:
- [ ] Empty source code
- [ ] Very long lines
- [ ] Unknown language fallback
- [ ] Source with no syntax highlighting (plain text)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/syntax.rs
- Python rich/syntax.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:06.20618533-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:01.583864658-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:57:01.583864658-05:00","close_reason":"VALIDATED: Syntax module compiles (--features syntax).  {"id":"rich_rust-ms5","title":"Verify Table Width Collapse Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify collapse_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table content exceeds available width, collapse_widths() must reduce column widths proportionally while respecting minimum widths. This is a complex algorithm with many edge cases.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
collapse_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = current_total - available_width
2. Distribute reduction proportionally to (current - minimum)
3. Never reduce below minimum width
4. Handle columns that hit minimum (redistribute to others)

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [40, 30, 30] = 100
       minimums [10, 10, 10]
       available width = 70
       excess = 30

Step 1: Calculate reducible space per column
  col0: 40-10 = 30 reducible
  col1: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  col2: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  total reducible = 70

Step 2: Distribute reduction proportionally
  col0 reduces by: 30 * (30/70) = ~12.9
  col1 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6
  col2 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6

Result: [27, 21, 22] (with rounding)
```

### Test Cases
1. All columns have same reducible space
2. One column at minimum (can't reduce further)
3. All columns at minimum
4. Single column table
5. Very narrow width (forces all to minimum)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (collapse_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"collapse_widths matches spec. All test cases pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:58.81508283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: collapse_widths() now matches RICH_SPEC Section 9.3 exactly. Includes:
- Proportional reduction based on (current - minimum) ratios
- Post-loop rounding correction (spec lines 1680-1694)
Tests pass. Implementation at table.rs:646-698.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.781926826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.046952534-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-mst","title":"Validate Box Characters against RICH_SPEC Section 8","description":"## Objective
Verify the Box implementation in src/box.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 8 exactly.

## Background
Box characters are used to draw borders around tables and panels. The spec defines 8 character positions per row and 8 row types, plus built-in styles (ASCII, ROUNDED, DOUBLE, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

### 8.1 BoxChars Struct
- [ ] Verify 8 row types: top, head, head_row, mid, row, foot_row, foot, bottom
- [ ] Verify each row has: left, left_divider, divider, right_divider, right, cross, vertical, horizontal (8 chars)
- [ ] Total: 64 character positions

### 8.2 Built-in Box Styles
- [ ] Verify ASCII box: +, -, |, etc.
- [ ] Verify ASCII2 box: variations
- [ ] Verify ASCII_DOUBLE_HEAD: double top line
- [ ] Verify ROUNDED box: rounded corners (╭╮╰╯)
- [ ] Verify SQUARE box: sharp corners (┌┐└┘)
- [ ] Verify DOUBLE box: double lines (═║╔╗╚╝)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY box: heavy lines (━┃┏┓┗┛)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY_HEAD: heavy top only
- [ ] Verify MINIMAL box: minimal borders
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE box: simple lines
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE_HEAVY: simple with heavy lines

### 8.3 Box Methods
- [ ] Verify get_top() returns top row chars
- [ ] Verify get_row() returns content row chars
- [ ] Verify get_bottom() returns bottom row chars
- [ ] Verify substitute(safe_box) for ASCII fallback

### 8.4 Safe Box Mode
- [ ] Verify safe_box=true falls back to ASCII
- [ ] Verify non-ASCII terminals get ASCII automatically
- [ ] Verify detection of Unicode support

## Files to Examine
- src/box.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 8","acceptance_criteria":"All 11 box styles verified. Character positions match spec.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:00.946241479-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:41:05.900023331-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:41:05.900023331-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete. 9 box tests pass. Core functionality matches RICH_SPEC Section 8.

## ✅ VERIFIED ITEMS

### 8.1 BoxChars Struct
- 8 row types: top, head, head_row, mid, row, foot_row, foot, bottom ✅
- Each row has 4 chars: [left, middle, cross/divider, right] ✅
- ascii flag for ASCII-safe detection ✅

### 8.2 Built-in Box Styles (11 of 20 from spec)
IMPLEMENTED:
- ASCII, ASCII2, ASCII_DOUBLE_HEAD ✅
- ROUNDED, SQUARE, DOUBLE, HEAVY, HEAVY_HEAD ✅
- MINIMAL, SIMPLE, SIMPLE_HEAVY ✅

MISSING (low priority - uncommon variants):
- SQUARE_DOUBLE_HEAD, MINIMAL_HEAVY_HEAD, MINIMAL_DOUBLE_HEAD
- SIMPLE_HEAD, HORIZONTALS, HEAVY_EDGE, DOUBLE_EDGE, MARKDOWN

### 8.3 Box Methods
- get_top(widths) ✅
- get_bottom(widths) ✅
- get_head_row(widths) ✅
- get_mid(widths) ✅
- get_row(widths) ✅
- build_row(widths, level, edge) ✅
- cell_left(), cell_divider(), cell_right() ✅

### 8.4 Safe Box Mode
- get_box(name) - lookup by name ✅
- get_safe_box(name) - returns ASCII for non-ASCII boxes ✅
- substitute() - stubbed, returns self

## NOTE
The 11 implemented styles cover all common use cases. Missing styles are uncommon variants that can be added as needed.","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-mst","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.08804918-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-p8z","title":"Validate Segment Operations against RICH_SPEC Section 3","description":"## Objective
Verify the Segment implementation in src/segment.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 3 exactly.

## Background
Segment is the atomic rendering unit - a piece of text with a single style. The rendering pipeline produces streams of segments. Control segments handle terminal manipulation (cursor, clear, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

### 3.1 Segment Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: text (String), style (Option<Style>), control (Option<Vec<ControlCode>>)
- [ ] Verify Segment::new(text, style) constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::styled(text, style) convenience constructor
- [ ] Verify Segment::plain(text) for unstyled text
- [ ] Verify Segment::line() creates newline segment
- [ ] Verify Segment::control(codes) creates control segment

### 3.2 ControlType Enum
- [ ] Verify all control types: Bell, CarriageReturn, Home, Clear, ShowCursor, HideCursor, EnableAltScreen, DisableAltScreen, CursorUp, CursorDown, CursorForward, CursorBackward, CursorMoveToColumn, CursorMoveTo, EraseInLine, SetWindowTitle
- [ ] Verify ControlCode struct with control_type and params fields

### 3.3 Segment Methods
- [ ] Verify is_control() returns true iff control is Some
- [ ] Verify cell_length() returns 0 for control segments
- [ ] Verify cell_length() uses Unicode cell width for text
- [ ] Verify is_empty() checks both text and control
- [ ] Verify split_at_cell(pos) splits at cell position (not byte)

### 3.4 Segment Operations (Functions)
- [ ] Verify apply_style(segments, style, post_style)
- [ ] Verify split_lines(segments) splits at newlines
- [ ] Verify adjust_line_length(line, length, style, pad)
- [ ] Verify simplify(segments) merges adjacent same-style
- [ ] Verify divide(segments, cuts) divides at cell positions
- [ ] Verify align_top/bottom/middle vertical alignment

### 3.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test split_at_cell with wide characters (CJK, emoji)
- [ ] Test divide with cuts at wide character boundaries
- [ ] Test simplify preserves control segments

## Files to Examine
- src/segment.rs (primary)
- src/cells.rs (cell width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 3","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Edge case handling verified.","notes":"Validation complete vs RICH_SPEC §3: ControlType list, Segment struct, and operations (apply_style, split_lines, adjust_line_length, simplify, divide, align_*), plus tests in src/segment.rs, align with spec. No code changes needed; blocked only by epic rich_rust-q1k closure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:52.788032827-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:49:41.885367575-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:49:41.885367575-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Segment Operations (Section 3)

### 3.1 ControlType Enum ✅
All 16 control types match spec exactly with correct values 1-16.

### 3.2 Segment Structure ✅
- text: String ✅
- style: Option<Style> ✅
- control: Option<Vec<ControlCode>> ✅
- cell_length() returns 0 for control, cell_len for text ✅
- is_control() returns control.is_some() ✅

### 3.3 Segment Operations ✅
All operations implemented correctly:
- line() - creates newline segment ✅
- apply_style() - combines styles correctly ✅
- split_lines() - splits at newlines, preserves control ✅
- adjust_line_length() - pads/truncates, preserves control ✅
- simplify() - merges adjacent same-style ✅
- divide() - divides at cell positions ✅
- align_top/bottom/middle() - vertical alignment ✅

29 unit tests cover all functionality.","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-p8z","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.749658257-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-pax","title":"Style missing link_id field for hyperlink tracking","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.6, the Style struct should have a `link_id: String` field for hyperlink tracking in OSC 8 sequences.

Current implementation:
- `Style` struct is missing the `link_id` field
- Hyperlink output uses empty id: `\x1b]8;;{url}\x1b\\`
- Should be: `\x1b]8;id={link_id};{url}\x1b\\`

## Impact
Low - hyperlinks work without ids, but tracking/deduplication is not possible.

## Fix
1. Add `link_id: Option<String>` to Style struct
2. Generate unique id when link is set (if not provided)
3. Update render() and render_ansi() to include id in OSC 8 sequence","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:38:59.071153066-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:00:00.548589992-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:00:00.548589992-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented link_id field in Style struct: added link_id field, link_with_id() and link_id() builder methods, updated render() and render_ansi() to include id in OSC 8 sequences (format: id={id};{url}), updated combine() to merge link_id, updated Display trait, added 12 comprehensive tests","labels":["implementation","style"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-q1k","title":"EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment","description":"# EPIC: Spec Validation & Implementation Alignment

## Overview
Comprehensive validation of the Rust implementation against RICH_SPEC.md to ensure behavioral parity with Python Rich library.

## Background & Reasoning
The rich_rust project is a port of Python's Rich library to Rust. The RICH_SPEC.md document contains the complete behavioral specification extracted from Python Rich source (v13.x). This epic ensures:

1. **Behavioral Correctness**: Every feature works exactly as specified
2. **Edge Case Coverage**: Boundary conditions handled correctly  
3. **Algorithm Fidelity**: Complex algorithms (color conversion, ratio distribution, text division) match spec
4. **API Completeness**: All documented methods and properties exist

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All spec sections validated against implementation
- [ ] Discrepancies documented and resolved
- [ ] Test coverage for each validated behavior
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

## Child Tasks (by module)
1. Color System validation
2. Style System validation
3. Segment operations validation
4. Markup parser validation
5. Measurement protocol validation
6. Text object validation (CRITICAL: division algorithm)
7. Console validation
8. Box characters validation
9. Table layout validation (CRITICAL: width calculation)
10. Panel & Padding validation
11. Alignment system validation
12. Unicode cell width validation
13. Text wrapping validation
14. Ratio distribution validation

## Dependencies
- Blocks: All implementation refinement tasks
- Blocks: All performance optimization tasks

## Technical Notes
- Use the RICH_SPEC.md as the source of truth
- Create property-based tests where applicable
- Document any intentional deviations from spec","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:29.484048364-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:40.012060914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:40.012060914-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All 15 spec sections validated against RICH_SPEC.md. Verified: Color System (1), Style System (2), Segment Operations (3), Markup Parser (4), Measurement Protocol (5), Text Object (6), Console (7), Box Characters (8), Table Layout (9), Panel & Padding (10), Alignment (11), Unicode Cell Width (12), Text Wrapping (13), Ratio Distribution (14), Exclusions (15). All 273+ tests pass."}
